summary: Lower anterior teeth are commonly involved due to developmental delays. Evidence suggests these areas may experience more pronounced periodontal disease in individuals with Down syndrome. Agent2 reasoned based on typical dental challenges associated with this condition but did not pinpoint specific tooth types beyond inferring involvement of lower anterior regions. Answer remains open as no definitive data was provided for exact teeth impacted severely by periodontitis.
